DMN.function: Self-referential processing, autobiographical memory, mind-wandering, social cognition, future thinking
DMN.ad_description: Most consistently disrupted network in AD; Aβ preferentially accumulates in DMN hubs (PCC, precuneus, mPFC); FC within DMN is primary imaging biomarker
DMN.progression: PCC-hippocampus FC reduction earliest; spreads to angular gyrus and mPFC in MCI; global DMN collapse in AD

SMN.function: Primary motor and somatosensory processing, motor planning, body representation
SMN.ad_description: Relatively preserved in early AD; affected in late-stage with motor symptoms; NC has higher SMN attention than disease stages
SMN.progression: Preserved in MCI; subtle changes in moderate AD; motor symptoms in late AD

VN.function: Primary and higher-order visual processing, visuospatial cognition, object/scene recognition
VN.ad_description: Shows U-shaped attention pattern: MCI has highest VN attention (compensatory hyperconnectivity), which collapses in AD; PCA variant shows early VN disruption
VN.progression: MCI: compensatory hyperconnectivity (VN attention highest); AD: FC collapse; PCA variant: primary VN disruption

SN.function: Salience detection, interoception, switching between DMN and FPN, emotional processing, autonomic regulation
SN.ad_description: Disrupted early in MCI; NC has significantly higher SN attention than MCI (SN=-0.32 in NC vs MCI); insula and ACC show early tau accumulation
SN.progression: MCI: insula FC disruption earliest SN change; modulates DMN-FPN switching impaired; AD: severe SN degradation

FPN.function: Goal-directed cognition, cognitive control, working memory, top-down attention, flexible task performance
FPN.ad_description: DLPFC-SPL circuit disrupted in MCI; executive function decline correlates with FPN FC reduction; FPN moderates DMN activity
FPN.progression: Subtle FC reduction in MCI between DLPFC and parietal; progressive working memory and executive dysfunction

LN.function: Episodic memory encoding/retrieval, emotional processing, fear conditioning, spatial navigation
LN.ad_description: Hippocampus-entorhinal circuit is primary AD pathology site (Braak Stage I-IV); LN attention is LOWEST in MCI (NC>>MCI), then rises in AD as damage becomes discriminative feature
LN.progression: Preclinical: entorhinal cortex tau; MCI: hippocampal FC most disrupted (lowest LN attention); AD: widespread limbic damage

VAN.function: Stimulus-driven attention reorienting, task-irrelevant salient detection, language-attention integration
VAN.ad_description: Disrupted in MCI affecting attention reorienting; shows positive attention difference in AD (AD>NC); VAN disruption linked to attentional lapses
VAN.progression: MCI: attention reorienting impaired; AD: further VAN dysregulation

BGN.function: Procedural learning, reward processing, motor sequence control, executive function via cortico-striatal loops
BGN.ad_description: Cortico-striatal FC changes in MCI; thalamo-cortical network involved in consciousness and gating; dopaminergic system relatively preserved in typical AD vs Lewy body
BGN.progression: Caudate-DLPFC FC reduction in MCI; thalamic connectivity changes; pallidal involvement in late AD

CereN.function: Motor coordination, cognitive processing (Crus I/II), balance, vestibular integration, timing
CereN.ad_description: Cognitive cerebellum (Crus I/II) FC changes with DMN and FPN in AD; motor cerebellum shows late-stage changes; gait and balance impairment in advanced AD
CereN.progression: Crus I/II show FC changes with prefrontal early; motor cerebellum affected in late-stage falls risk
